title: Following a hearing by the Senate Appropriations Committee, six of Assemblymember Alex Lee’s legislation are headed to the Senate Floor.
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Social housing developments in California will be spurred by AB 309.
Social housing is publicly backed, self-sustaining housing that accommodates a mix of household income ranges.
Daylighting improves traffic safety by making it easier for everyone on the street to see oncoming traffic.
AB 413 aims to reduce traffic collisions by keeping 20 feet of an intersection or crosswalk’s approach side clear of stopped vehicles.
California’s pedestrian fatality rate is nearly 25% higher than the national average.
AB 652 will enable communities disproportionately impacted by pesticide exposure to participate in decision-making processes about pesticide use.
The Environmental Justice Advisory Committee in the Department of Pesticide Regulation will recommend ways to integrate environmental justice into policies and programs.
AB 664 will ensure that renters served solely by their wells have access to potable water in California.
Every person in California has the legal right to clean, safe, and affordable water.
AB 1490 will provide incentives to qualified affordable housing developments that repurpose existing buildings for housing.
Adaptive reuse is a faster and less expensive method of creating housing units.
